title: andropogon sorghum var. cafer
intro: Andropogon sorghum var. cafer, also known as Indian sago or wild sorghum, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Indian sago is often prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or oil, and can be consumed internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
